reasoning: The Lean statement faithfully captures the informal claim. `hp : Nat.Prime p` encodes that p is prime, `q : Polynomial (ZMod p)` with `hq : Irreducible q` and `hn : q.degree = n` encodes that q is an irreducible polynomial over F = ℤ_p of degree n. The conclusion has two parts joined by conjunction: (i) `∃ is_fin : Fintype (Polynomial (ZMod p) ⧸ span {q}), @card ... is_fin = p ^ n` asserts the quotient is a finite type with exactly p^n elements (Fintype.card is canonical so the existential witness is merely the standard way to express finiteness + cardinality); (ii) `IsField (Polynomial (ZMod p) ⧸ span {q})` asserts the quotient is a field. With `Ideal` opened, `span {q}` is `Ideal.span {q}`, giving the quotient F[x]/(q(x)). Both conclusions together match "is a field having exactly p^n elements" exactly, with no extra or missing hypotheses.
